analysis.summary: Odoo is a comprehensive web-based ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system built on a modular architecture with a plugin-based addon system. It provides integrated business applications including CRM, accounting, inventory management, e-commerce, and human resources. The system follows a monolithic Python architecture with PostgreSQL persistence, web controllers for HTTP endpoints, and extensive localization support across multiple languages and countries.
capabilities: Processes OAuth2 authentication flows with state management and provider integration (addons/auth_oauth/controllers/main.py)
Implements WebAuthn passkey authentication for passwordless login (addons/auth_passkey/controllers/main.py)
Manages user signup workflows with email confirmation and account validation (addons/auth_signup/controllers/main.py)
Enforces session timeout policies with identity verification prompts (addons/auth_timeout/controllers/main.py)
Executes webhook-based automation rules with HTTP endpoint routing (addons/base_automation/controllers/main.py)
Handles file imports for data migration and CSV processing (addons/base_import/controllers/main.py)
Supports module installation via ZIP file upload with authentication (addons/base_import_module/controllers/main.py)
Provides system setup dashboard with user statistics and demo data management (addons/base_setup/controllers/main.py)
Creates customizable dashboards with drag-and-drop action widgets (addons/board/controllers/main.py)
Implements real-time messaging bus for notifications and model updates (addons/bus/controllers/main.py)
Manages calendar events with meeting acceptance/decline via token-based access (addons/calendar/controllers/main.py)
Processes accounting transactions with journal entries and reconciliation (addons/account/models/account_move.py)
Handles multi-currency operations with exchange rate management (addons/account/models/res_currency.py)
Implements tax calculation engine with configurable tax rules and repartition (addons/account/models/account_tax.py)
Manages payment processing with multiple payment methods and terms (addons/account/models/account_payment.py)
Provides chart of accounts templates for different countries and regulations (addons/account/models/chart_template.py)
Supports bank statement import and automatic reconciliation (addons/account/models/account_bank_statement.py)
Implements analytic accounting with cost centers and project tracking (addons/account/models/account_analytic_account.py)
Generates financial reports with customizable layouts and filters (addons/account/report/account_invoice_report.py)
Manages partner relationships with customer and supplier information (addons/account/models/partner.py)
Handles product catalog management with pricing and cost tracking (addons/account/models/product.py)
Implements data migration scripts for version upgrades (migration files in addons/*/migrations/)
Provides extensive localization support for 80+ countries (addons/l10n_* directories)
Supports IoT device integration with specialized hardware drivers (addons/iot_box_image/configuration/)
Implements role-based access control with security groups and rules (addons/account/security/account_security.xml)
use_cases: Small to large enterprises use Odoo as their primary ERP system to manage accounting, sales, inventory, and operations
Manufacturing companies use it to track production workflows, bills of materials, and quality control processes
E-commerce businesses use it to manage online stores, product catalogs, and order fulfillment
Service companies use it for project management, time tracking, and customer relationship management
Multi-national corporations use it for consolidated financial reporting across different countries and currencies
Developers and system integrators use it as a platform to build custom business applications using the addon framework
analysis.target_audience: Business users, system administrators, developers, and consultants working with enterprise business applications
technical_stack: PostgreSQL (implied from ORM models and database migrations)
XML for data definitions and views (extensive .xml files)
api_endpoints: POST /base_import/set_file — File upload for data import (addons/base_import/controllers/main.py)
POST /base_import_module/login_upload — Module installation via ZIP upload (addons/base_import_module/controllers/main.py)
POST /bus/get_model_definitions — Model metadata for real-time updates (addons/bus/controllers/main.py)
integrations: Google services integration for calendar, Gmail, and address autocomplete (addons/google_*/)
Cloud storage providers: Azure, Google Cloud (addons/cloud_storage_*/)
Payment processors and banking systems (account payment models)
Email servers for SMTP/IMAP communication (mail system)
GeoIP location services (geoip2 dependency in requirements.txt)

components: Core framework providing ORM, HTTP routing, and security
Addon system for modular business applications
Database abstraction layer with PostgreSQL backend
Reporting engine with PDF and Excel generation
architecture.data_flow: HTTP requests are routed through Werkzeug to controller methods, which interact with ORM models that persist data in PostgreSQL. Business logic is implemented in model methods, with UI updates pushed via the bus system for real-time notifications.
architecture.persistence: PostgreSQL database with SQLAlchemy-style ORM, migration scripts for schema updates, and XML data files for initial configuration
architecture.concurrency_model: Gevent-based async processing with greenlet coroutines for handling concurrent requests
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST for web API, JSONRPC for rich client communication, WebSocket-like bus system for real-time updates, webhook support for external integrations
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Addon-based architecture where each addon in addons/ directory provides models, views, controllers, and business logic that extend core functionality

storage_backends: File system for attachments and static assets
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational model with extensive use of foreign keys, inheritance via delegation, and polymorphic relationships through reference fields
data_model.migration_strategy: Python migration scripts in addons/*/migrations/ directories with pre-migrate, post-migrate, and end-migrate hooks for version upgrades

algorithms.problem_type: Enterprise resource planning with multi-tenancy, complex business workflows, and real-time data synchronization
algorithms.complexity_indicators: High complexity evident from 46,612 files, extensive addon ecosystem, multi-language support (80+ locales), and complex financial calculation engines

security_model.authentication: Multi-method authentication including passwords, OAuth2, LDAP, WebAuthn passkeys, and TOTP
security_model.authorization: Role-based access control (RBAC) with security groups, record rules, and field-level permissions defined in XML security files
security_model.data_protection: Cryptography library for encryption, secure session management, CSRF protection, and access token-based API authentication
security_model.security_headers: CSRF token validation, secure session cookies, and XSS protection through template escaping
security_model.vulnerability_handling: Security policy documented (SECURITY.md), responsible disclosure process, and regular security updates

deployment.environment_config: Configuration through Python config files, command-line arguments, and environment variables as typical for Python applications
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling through multiple worker processes, database replication support

code_quality_signals.test_framework: Python unittest (implied from test file structure)
code_quality_signals.code_style: flake8 configuration in setup.cfg with RST documentation standards

project_health.recent_activity: Recent activity shows bug fixes in Polish localization for EDI access control
project_health.community_signals: Strong community health with CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, and comprehensive documentation

information_gaps: Database schema details not fully visible from provided migration files
Frontend architecture details limited by code sample truncation
CI/CD pipeline configuration not present in provided files

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: The system is fundamentally a plugin host with an addon-based architecture where each addon in addons/ directory provides models, views, controllers, and business logic that extend core functionality. The core framework provides ORM, HTTP routing, and security while business applications are implemented as modular addons.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: Extensive ORM model definitions with foreign keys and relationships, XML security files defining field-level permissions, and migration scripts for schema evolution indicate a highly structured data contract
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from Active Record ORM pattern, account.move model for transaction management, and extensive database operations across models. Core operations are Create/Read/Update/Delete with complex business logic layered on top
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from PostgreSQL backend, account reconciliation algorithms, and financial transaction processing which requires transactional consistency for business operations
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Manages a hierarchical registry of pluggable business rule modules that operate on shared entity graphs with role-based access control and real-time state synchronization across multiple client sessions.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: HTTP request → controller routing → ORM model interaction → database persistence → real-time bus notification → client update, with plugin modules intercepting and extending each stage
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The plugin host architecture, ORM framework, and role-based access control system are highly generic and could be repurposed for any domain requiring modular business applications. The core framework abstracts away domain-specific concerns into addons. However, the accounting-specific models and financial calculations are domain-locked. The real-time bus system, authentication framework, and internationalization capabilities are broadly reusable.

evidence_claims.claim: System implements a plugin-based addon architecture
evidence_claims.evidence: Plugin/Extension pattern for addons with each addon in addons/ directory providing models, views, controllers

evidence_claims.claim: Uses PostgreSQL for ACID-compliant data persistence
evidence_claims.evidence: PostgreSQL backend mentioned in architecture with migration scripts for schema updates

evidence_claims.claim: Implements real-time notifications via bus system
evidence_claims.evidence: addons/bus/controllers/main.py provides real-time messaging bus for notifications and model updates

evidence_claims.claim: Implements role-based access control with security groups
evidence_claims.evidence: Role-based access control (RBAC) with security groups and rules mentioned in addons/account/security/account_security.xml

market_reach.primary_industry: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) / Business Management Software
cross_industry_applications: Healthcare Management Systems - patient records, appointments, billing modules as plugins
Educational Institution Management - student records, course management, grading as modular addons
Government Administrative Systems - citizen services, permit processing, tax collection as separate modules
Real Estate Management Platforms - property listings, tenant management, maintenance tracking as plugins
Manufacturing Execution Systems - production planning, quality control, inventory as modular components
Non-profit Organization Management - donor management, volunteer coordination, grant tracking as addons
Professional Services Automation - project management, time tracking, client billing as plugin modules
Supply Chain Management - vendor management, procurement, logistics tracking as modular applications

friction_report.migration_path: Extract core framework components (ORM, plugin system, security framework) into a standalone platform, replace domain-specific models with generic entity types, create new addon templates for target domain

comparable_systems: Salesforce Platform - plugin-based CRM with custom objects and workflow automation
Drupal CMS - modular content management with hook system and entity management
Jenkins CI/CD - plugin architecture for build automation and deployment pipelines
Moodle LMS - modular learning management with course plugins and activity modules
ServiceNow - workflow automation platform with custom applications and ITSM modules
